prs.title: [ROCm][Perf] Replace WNA16 MoE Triton kernel with FlyDSL MoE
prs.author: amd-asalykov
prs.body_head: ## Purpose This PR replaces WNA16 MoE GPTQ Triton kernel with FlyDSL MoE kernel if 1. weights are 4-bit quantized 2. group_size==-1 or 32 targeting Kimi K2.5 INT4 model ## Test Plan Measure e2e perf. and test accuracy on gsm8k ## Test Result Accuracy: ~0.94 Perf: ---

prs.title: [Core] Add `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` env var
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
vLLM now uses asynchronous scheduling by default and in the majority of cases. Performance relies on the absence of any gpu<->cpu synchronizations on the main cuda stream, but such syncs can be opaque and it is easy for them to creep in accidentally.

This change adds a `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` which enables `torch.cuda.set_sync_debug_mode` for the model forward pass and sampler, so that we can easily check for such syncs.

I'm trying first to enable it globally in the CI to flush out syncs that need to be fixed or where they are unavoidable and the check needs to be suppressed. Will then probably split the fixes into separate PR(s).

prs.title: [ROCm][FEAT] AITER Fused Allreduce + RMSNorm
prs.author: vllmellm
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

This PR integrates AITER fused all-reduce + RMSNorm kernel for ROCm platforms, 

introduces:
-  `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` that fuses the all-reduce collective with the subsequent RMSNorm (and fused-add-RMSNorm) 

AITER's `v0.1.10.post3` version exposes tw0 new function `custom_fused_ar_rms` and `custom_fused_ar_rms_quant` in `CustomAllReduce` class. In this PR we are only utilizing `custom_fused_ar_rms` from AITER's CustomAllReduce only during fusion pass pattern matching. 

###  Changes

in `vllm/_aiter_ops.py`:

- `AiterCustomAllreduceProto`: typed Protocol for the AITER `CustomAllreduce` object 
- `rocm_aiter_ops.initialize_aiter_allreduce(group, device)`: initializes the AITER all-reduce communicator.
- `rocm_aiter_ops.get_aiter_allreduce()` / `destroy_aiter_allreduce()`: lifecycle management
- `torch.ops.vllm.rocm_aiter_fused_allreduce_rmsnorm`: registered custom op with fake impl for `torch.compile`

in (`vllm/compilation/passes/pass_manager.py`):

When `fuse_allreduce_rms` is enabled and `rocm_aiter_ops.is_enabled()` is `True`, `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` is used instead of `AllReduceFusionPass`. CUDA behavior is unchanged.

in (`vllm/config/vllm.py`):

On ROCm, `fuse_allreduce_rms` is now enabled automatically when:
- `VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1`
- RMSNorm AITER ops are enabled
- `tensor_parallel_size > 1`

in `vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py`:

The AITER `CustomAllreduce.capture()` context manager is included alongside the existing `ca_comm.capture()` during CUDA graph capture, so the fused kernel is correctly captured.

## Tests

- `tests/compile/passes/distributed/test_fusion_all_reduce.py`: extended to test `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` on ROCm; FP8/FP4 quant fusion tests are skipped on ROCm (not yet supported)
- `tests/compile/fusions_e2e/test_tp2_ar_rms.py`: `test_tp2_ar_rms_fusions` now runs on both CUDA and ROCm with ROCm attention backends (`ROCM_ATTN`, `ROCM_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN`)
- `.buildk

prs.title: [ROCm] Cast score correction bias tensor during model construction for DeepSeek/Kimi-K2
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head: ## Purpose The moe score correction bias tensor was being cast to the gate output dtype on every forward pass. The datatype that this tensor needs to be cast to is known at model construction time and never changes beyond that. So this repeated cast is redundant work that launches an extra GPU kernel per MoE layer per forward call. This PR moves the cast to the model construction thereby eliminating the per-forward-pass overhead. ## Summary ### Before ### After The trace shows the elementwise operation kernel responsible for this typecast operation happening before the grouped-topk operation in every forward pass. With the following changes this typecast is shifted to model construction thereby avoiding the call during every forward pass: - **`vllm/model_executor/models/deepseek_v2.py`**: Pre-cast `e_score_correction_bias` to match `gate.out_dtype` during `DeepseekV2MoE` construction. Since all downstream consumers (FusedMoE, router) share the same `nn.Parameter` object, this single mutation propagates everywhere. - **`vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/rocm_aiter_fused_moe.py`**: Replace the runtime `.to()` cast with an assert that the bias dtype already matches the gating output dtype, catching any future regression where the init-time cast is missed. - **`vllm/model_executor/layers/fused_moe/router/fused_topk_bias_router.py`**: Same change — replace the runtime `.to()` cast with a matching assert. ## Test Result ### Accuracy Benchmark | Metric | Score | Threshold | Status -- | -- | -- | -- | -- GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (flexible-extract) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (strict-match) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS ### Performance |

prs.title: [ROCm] Implement GPU-to-NUMA-node detection
prs.author: pschlan-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Implement automatic NUMA topology detection on ROCm platforms via the amdsmi library (which is already a dependency).

## Test Plan
Run vLLM with `--numa-bind`, observe correct topology detection

## Test Result
Topology is detected correctly on the test system with 8 GPUs connected to 2 NUMA nodes:
```INFO 04-16 12:57:41 [numa_utils.py:111] Auto-detected NUMA nodes for GPUs: [0, 0, 0, 0, 1, 1, 1, 1]```

prs.title: Make v1 KV cache initialization messages device-neutral
prs.author: whk-lab
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Fix GPU-specific init-time KV cache reporting in `v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py` for shared initialization paths that are also used by non-GPU backends.

The KV cache memory accounting in this path already operates on generic `available_memory`, and CPU workers also feed into the same path via `determine_available_memory()`. However, several logs and errors still hardcode GPU-specific wording such as `GPU KV cache size` and `available GPU memory`, which is misleading on non-GPU backends.

## Test Plan
Ran the following locally:
```bash
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py -v
.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py -k "report_kv_cache_config_uses_device_neutral_messages" -v
.venv/bin/pre-commit run --files vllm/v1/core/kv_cache_utils.py tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py
```
## Test Result

- tests/v1/engine/test_init_error_messaging.py: 2 passed
- tests/v1/core/test_kv_cache_utils.py -k "report_kv_cache_config_uses_device_neutral_messages": 1 passed
- pre-commit on changed files: passed

On macOS arm64, uv pip install -r requirements/test.in could not be fully resolved because runai-model-streamer==0.15.7 does not provide compatible wheels for this platform, so I used a minimal local environment to run the targeted tests above.

prs.title: [ROCm] Add gfx1102/gfx1103 support
prs.author: mgehre-amd
prs.body_head:
gfx1103 (RDNA 3, e.g. Radeon 780M iGPU) was missing from the HIP_SUPPORTED_ARCHS list in CMakeLists.txt and from the compile-time architecture macros in skinny_gemms.cu and attention.cu.

The skinny GEMM and attention kernels defined __HIP__GFX1X__ and __HIP__GFX11__ by listing individual arch targets (__gfx1100__, __gfx1101__, __gfx1150__, __gfx1151__) but missed __gfx1103__. This caused wvSplitK kernel bodies to compile as UNREACHABLE_CODE (assert false) on gfx1103 while the wave32 reduction code was still active, resulting in a crash at runtime.

Replace __HIP__GFX11__ / __HIP__GFX12__ with the compiler-provided __GFX11__ / __GFX12__ umbrella macros which are defined for every target in each family. This avoids needing to enumerate individual targets and automatically covers future gfx11xx/gfx12xx additions.
